title: लक्ष्मी मङ्गलाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री लक्ष्मी मङ्गलाष्टकम् ॥

हरिणीं सरसीरुहासनस्थां गजपृष्ठोपरि संस्थिताम्।
नमामि श्रियं विष्णुपत्नीं मङ्गलं वरलक्ष्म्यै नमः॥१॥

पद्मप्रिये पद्मिनि पद्महस्ते पद्मालये पद्मदलायताक्षि।
विश्वप्रिये विष्णुमनोऽनुकूले मङ्गलं श्रीमहालक्ष्म्यै नमः॥२॥

क्षीरोदसम्भवे कमले कमलायताक्षि।
श्रीविष्णुहृत्कमलवासिनि विश्वमातः।
क्षीरोदजे कमलकोमलगर्भगौरि मङ्गलं श्रीमहालक्ष्म्यै नमः॥३॥

सर्वमङ्गलमाङ्गल्ये विष्णुवक्षःस्थलालये।
मङ्गलार्थं मङ्गलेशि मङ्गलं श्रीमहालक्ष्म्यै नमः॥४॥

हिरण्यवर्णां हरिणीं सुवर्णरजतस्रजाम्।
चन्द्रां हिरण्मयीं लक्ष्मीं मङ्गलं जातवेदसे नमः॥५॥

धनलक्ष्मि धान्यलक्ष्मि सन्तानलक्ष्मि गजलक्ष्मि।
धैर्यलक्ष्मि विजयलक्ष्मि मङ्गलं अष्टलक्ष्म्यै नमः॥६॥

आदिलक्ष्मि विद्यालक्ष्मि भाग्यलक्ष्मि प्रभो।
सिद्धिलक्ष्मि चारुरूपे मङ्गलं सर्वलक्ष्म्यै नमः॥७॥

यशोलक्ष्मि वीरलक्ष्मि सर्वकामप्रदायिनि।
सौभाग्यदायिनि श्रीदे मङ्गलं वरलक्ष्म्यै नमः॥८॥

मङ्गलाष्टकमेतद्यो लक्ष्म्या भक्त्या सदा पठेत्।
सर्वसम्पत्समृद्धिः स्यात् लक्ष्मीवासो भवेत् सदा॥

॥ इति श्री लक्ष्मी मङ्गलाष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Lakshmi Mangalashtakam ||

Harinim Sarasiruhasthanam Gajaprishtopari Samsthitam.
Namami Shriyam Vishnu-patnim Mangalam Varalakshmyai Namah.||1||

Padmapriye Padmini Padmahaste Padmalaye Padmadalayatakshi.
Vishvapriye Vishnumano-nukoole Mangalam Shri Mahalakshmyai Namah.||2||

Kshirodasambhave Kamale Kamalayatakshi.
Shri Vishnu-hritkamala-vasini Vishvamatah.
Kshirodaje Kamala-komala-garbha-gauri Mangalam Shri Mahalakshmyai Namah.||3||

Sarvamangala-mangalye Vishnu-vakshah-sthalaalaye.
Mangalartham Mangaleshi Mangalam Shri Mahalakshmyai Namah.||4||

Hiranyavarnam Harinim Suvarna-rajata-srajam.
Chandram Hiranmayim Lakshmim Mangalam Jatavedase Namah.||5||

Dhanalakshmi Dhanyalakshmi Santanalakshmi Gajalakshmi.
Dhairyalakshmi Vijayalakshmi Mangalam Ashtalakshmyai Namah.||6||

Adilakshmi Vidyalakshmi Bhagyalakshmi Prabho.
Siddhilakshmi Charuroope Mangalam Sarvalakshmyai Namah.||7||

Yasholakshmi Viralakshmi Sarvakamapradayini.
Saubhagyadayini Shride Mangalam Varalakshmyai Namah.||8||

Mangalashtakam-Etadyo Lakshmya Bhaktya Sada Pathet.
Sarva-sampat-samriddhih Syat Lakshmi-vaso Bhavet Sada.

|| Iti Shri Lakshmi Mangalashtakam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
१. कमलासन पर विराजमान, गजपृष्ठ पर स्थित, विष्णुपत्नी श्री लक्ष्मी को मंगल नमस्कार।

२. पद्मप्रिया, पद्मिनी, पद्महस्ता, पद्मालया, पद्मनयनी, विश्वप्रिया, विष्णुमनोऽनुकूला महालक्ष्मी को मंगल।

३. क्षीरसागर से प्रकट, कमलनयनी, विष्णुहृदयकमलवासिनी, विश्वमाता, कमल-कोमल-गौरी महालक्ष्मी को मंगल।

४. सर्वमंगलमांगल्ये, विष्णुवक्षस्थलवासिनी, मंगलेशी महालक्ष्मी को मंगल।

५. स्वर्णवर्णा, हरिणी, सुवर्ण-रजत माला वाली, चन्द्रमुखी, हिरण्मयी लक्ष्मी को मंगल।

६. धनलक्ष्मी, धान्यलक्ष्मी, सन्तानलक्ष्मी, गजलक्ष्मी, धैर्यलक्ष्मी, विजयलक्ष्मी — अष्टलक्ष्मी को मंगल।

७. आदिलक्ष्मी, विद्यालक्ष्मी, भाग्यलक्ष्मी, सिद्धिलक्ष्मी, चारुरूपा — सर्वलक्ष्मी को मंगल।

८. यशोलक्ष्मी, वीरलक्ष्मी, सर्वकामप्रदायिनी, सौभाग्यदायिनी श्रीदा वरलक्ष्मी को मंगल।

जो भक्तिपूर्वक इस लक्ष्मी मंगलाष्टक का नित्य पाठ करता है, उसे सर्वसम्पत्ति की समृद्धि होती है और लक्ष्मी सदा उसके गृह में वास करती हैं।
benefits: Invokes the eight forms of Lakshmi for complete prosperity
Traditionally associated with wealth, fortune, and auspiciousness in all endeavors
Traditionally recited to invoke lakshmi's permanent residence in the devotee's home
Ideal for recitation during Varalakshmi Vrata and Diwali
Invokes all eight forms of prosperity — ashta lakshmi blessings
Traditionally recited for removal of financial difficulties and poverty
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Lakshmi, Lakshmi Mangalashtakam (लक्ष्मी मङ्गलाष्टकम्) stands as an eight-verse devotional hymn. Attributed to Lakshmi Tantra, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to ensure Lakshmi's permanent residence in the devotee's home as well as grant all eight forms of prosperity — Ashta Lakshmi blessings. Additionally, it is known to remove financial difficulties and poverty. This sacred text is ideally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and during Diwali, Varalakshmi Vrata, and Sharad Purnima for enhanced blessings. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Lakshmi Mangalashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Lakshmi Mangalashtakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight auspicious verses invoking Goddess Lakshmi for wealth and prosperity.
relatedSearches: Lakshmi Mangalashtakam in Sanskrit lyrics PDF
Lakshmi Mangalashtak benefits for wealth prosperity
Ashta Lakshmi names Dhana Dhanya Santana Gaja
Hiranya varnam harinim Suvarna Rajata Srajam

seoFaqs.question: What is the Lakshmi Mangalashtakam and what is its source?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lakshmi Mangalashtakam (लक्ष्मी मङ्गलाष्टकम् — mangala = auspicious, ashtakam = eight-verse hymn) is an eight-verse devotional stotra to Goddess Mahalakshmi in which each verse concludes with the refrain Mangalam Shri Mahalakshmyai Namah — All auspiciousness be to Sri Mahalakshmi. It is preserved in the classical Lakshmi Tantra and Vaishnava paddhati tradition and is recited in conjunction with the Shri Suktam, Lakshmi Ashtottara Shatanamavali, and Kanakadhara Stotram during major Lakshmi pujas. The mangalashtaka genre — the eight auspicious verses — is a specific liturgical form recited at the conclusion of pujas to invoke complete and lasting auspiciousness on the worshipper, the household, and all beings. (Sources: Lakshmi Tantra; Vaishnava paddhati; classical stotra-sangraha tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Padmapriye Padmini Padmahaste Padmalaye Padmadalayatakshi?
seoFaqs.answer: This celebrated second verse of the Lakshmi Mangalashtakam is one of the most quoted Lakshmi shlokas in the entire tradition, addressing the Goddess by six lotus epithets in a single verse: Padmapriye — beloved of the lotus; Padmini — the lotus-goddess herself; Padmahaste — holding lotuses in her hands; Padmalaye — dwelling in the lotus; Padmadalayatakshi — with eyes long like lotus petals; Vishvapriye Vishnu-mano-anukoole — beloved of the universe and ever-pleasing to Vishnu's mind. The lotus (padma) is Lakshmi's defining symbol — it represents her birth from the Kshira-sagara at the churning of the cosmic ocean, her purity rising untouched above the waters, and the unfolding of all beauty, abundance and grace. (Sources: Lakshmi Tantra; Vishnu Purana; Vaishnava paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: Which forms of Lakshmi are invoked in the sixth and seventh verses?
seoFaqs.answer: The sixth and seventh verses of the Lakshmi Mangalashtakam invoke the celebrated Ashta-Lakshmi (eight forms of Lakshmi) and additional aspects: (Verse 6) Dhana-Lakshmi (wealth), Dhanya-Lakshmi (grain and food), Santana-Lakshmi (progeny), Gaja-Lakshmi (royal abundance with elephants), Dhairya-Lakshmi (courage), Vijaya-Lakshmi (victory) — concluding Mangalam Ashta-Lakshmyai Namah; (Verse 7) Adi-Lakshmi (the primordial Lakshmi, the source of all forms), Vidya-Lakshmi (the goddess of knowledge), Bhagya-Lakshmi (fortune), Siddhi-Lakshmi (spiritual accomplishment), Charu-rupa (the beautiful one) — concluding Mangalam Sarva-Lakshmyai Namah. Together with the Yasho-Lakshmi (fame) and Vira-Lakshmi (heroism) of verse 8, the stotra invokes the complete spectrum of feminine cosmic abundance. (Sources: Ashta-Lakshmi Stotra tradition; Lakshmi Tantra; Vaishnava paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Lakshmi Mangalashtakam most auspicious to recite?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lakshmi Mangalashtakam is most powerful when recited on: (1) every Friday (Shukravar — Lakshmi's day, ruled by Shukra-graha governing wealth, beauty and abundance); (2) Diwali / Lakshmi Puja night (Kartik Krishna Amavasya — the supreme Lakshmi festival when she enters every home blessed with cleanliness, deepams and devotion); (3) Varalakshmi Vrata (Shravana Shukla Friday before Purnima — the great Lakshmi vrat for married women in South India); (4) Sharad Purnima (Ashwin Purnima — when Lakshmi descends and asks Ko jagarti — who is awake?); (5) Akshaya Tritiya (Vaishakha Shukla Tritiya — for new ventures, gold purchase and lasting wealth); (6) every Margashirsha Thursday (Lakshmi Vara — South Indian tradition); (7) Dhanteras (Trayodashi before Diwali — for Dhanvantari-Lakshmi worship); (8) at the inauguration of any new business, home or financial venture. (Sources: Drikpanchang Lakshmi calendar; Vaishnava paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Lakshmi Mangalashtakam and Lakshmi Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The two stotras are distinct though both have eight verses and address Goddess Lakshmi: (1) the Lakshmi Mangalashtakam is structurally a mangala-stotra — each of its eight verses ends with the refrain Mangalam Shri Mahalakshmyai Namah, making it a benediction stotra recited at the conclusion of pujas to invoke auspiciousness; its mood is celebratory and benedictory; (2) the Lakshmi Ashtakam (also called Mahalakshmi Ashtakam, beginning Namaste-stu Mahamaye Shri-pithe Sura-pujite, attributed to Indra in the Padma Purana) is a praise-stotra recited at the start or core of puja; its mood is supplicatory, asking for protection, victory and prosperity. The Mangalashtakam is recited specifically when concluding worship, after arati and naivedya, to seal in the blessings; the Ashtakam is recited during the main puja sequence. (Sources: Padma Purana; Lakshmi Tantra; classical stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the phalashruti (fruit of recitation) of the Lakshmi Mangalashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The concluding phalashruti verse declares: Mangalashtakam-etadyo Lakshmya bhaktya sada pathet, sarva-sampat-samriddhih syat Lakshmi-vaso bhavet sada — Whoever recites this Mangalashtakam to Lakshmi daily with devotion attains the prosperity of all wealth, and Lakshmi takes up permanent residence in their home. Additional traditional phala include: removal of poverty (daridrya-nashana) and accumulated debt; success in business, agriculture, profession and finance; harmony in the household and prevention of family quarrels; protection of wealth from theft, fire and depletion; bestowal of all eight Ashta-Lakshmi blessings together; the supreme phala of attaining Vishnu's eternal abode (Vaikuntha-prapti) at the feet of Lakshmi-Narayana. (Sources: Lakshmi Mangalashtakam phalashruti; Lakshmi Tantra; Vaishnava paddhati.)
